title: Redundant forward declarations after function definitions
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshcore/adapter.c
Line: 563-565

```c
static int preflight_tx_slots(struct lichen_meshcore_adapter *adapter,
                  uint32_t needed);
static uint8_t meshcore_error_from_errno(int err);
```

These are forward declarations, but meshcore_error_from_errno was already defined at line 420-443. The forward declaration of preflight_tx_slots is valid (defined later at line 840), but having a mix of pre-definition and post-definition forward declarations suggests disorganized code structure.

While not a runtime bug, this is confusing and violates the single-definition-per-entity principle. The forward declaration of meshcore_error_from_errno is dead code.

Fix: Remove the redundant forward declaration of meshcore_error_from_errno at line 565.
close_reason: Redundant forward declaration removed; code cleaned; test coverage via meshcore_adapter ZTESTs unaffected
